title: alstonia costata var. fragrans
intro: Alstonia costata var. fragrans, also known as the Fragrant Milkwood or Malabar Thorn, is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Alstonia costata var. fragrans include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate cough and sore throat, and even treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, powder, or oil, and is used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to treat a range of ailments, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin problems.
